title: Daily Market Digest: Geopolitical Tensions & Inflation Fears Drive Broad Sell-Off
subtitle: March 19, 2026

items.headline: Global Markets Reel as Geopolitical Conflicts Intensify and Fed Warns on Inflation
items.summary: U.S. equity futures pointed lower this morning, reflecting a continuation of the negative sentiment that saw Wall Street fall sharply on Wednesday. Escalating geopolitical conflicts between the U.S.-Israel force and Iran, coupled with a hawkish stance from the Federal Reserve regarding persistent inflation, have sent crude oil prices soaring and triggered a broad-based sell-off across global markets. The CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) surged, indicating heightened investor fear.

items.statContext: Lost 1.6% (-768.11 points), marking its lowest close for 2026.

items.headline: Swarmer Surges on Strong Performance
items.summary: Swarmer led the list of top gainers with a significant jump.

items.headline: Artelo Biosciences Sees Major Gains
items.summary: Artelo Biosciences experienced substantial price appreciation during today's trading.

items.headline: AIM ImmunoTech Rallies
items.summary: AIM ImmunoTech was among the day's strong performers, posting notable gains.

items.headline: Five Below Posts Strong Earnings, Stock Jumps
items.summary: Five Below shares surged after the retailer reported a strong Q4 beat and issued positive guidance for Q1 and FY26.

items.headline: Rocket Lab Declines Following Equity Offering
items.summary: Rocket Lab's stock dropped significantly after the company completed a follow-on equity offering.

items.headline: Carvana Experiences Downturn
items.summary: Carvana shares declined as the broader market faced selling pressure.

items.headline: Strategy Inc. Tumbles
items.summary: Strategy Inc., a Bitcoin treasury company, saw its stock price tumble.

items.headline: Micron Shares Fall Despite Strong Earnings
items.summary: Micron Technology's shares fell despite the chipmaker beating fiscal Q2 sales and earnings, likely due to increased capital expenditure guidance.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady Amid Elevated Uncertainty
items.summary: The U.S. Federal Reserve maintained its benchmark interest rate in the range of 3.5% to 3.75% for the second consecutive time, following its March 18th FOMC meeting. The central bank raised its 2026 headline PCE inflation forecast to 2.7% (from 2.4%) and core PCE to 2.7% (from 2.5%), while modestly upgrading GDP growth to 2.4%. Officials now project only one rate cut of 25 basis points for 2026, adopting a 'wait-and-see' approach due to geopolitical risks and inflation concerns.

items.headline: Oil Prices Skyrocket as Middle East Tensions Escalate
items.summary: Crude oil prices surged above $110-$116 per barrel following intensified geopolitical conflicts between the U.S.-Israel force and Iran. Attacks on energy infrastructure in the Gulf region have fueled supply disruption fears, contributing significantly to global inflation worries and market instability.

items.headline: Energy Sector Leads, Healthcare Lags Amid Broad Market Weakness
items.summary: In a challenging market, the Energy Select Sector SPDR (XLE) managed to climb 1.1%, positioning it as a leading sector. Conversely, the Health Care Select Sector SPDR (XLV) fell 0.9%, with eight out of eleven S&P 500 sectors ending in negative territory. Consumer Discretionary is also notably underweight by fund managers.

items.headline: February PPI Rises Above Expectations, Signaling Persistent Inflation
items.summary: The Department of Labor reported that the Producer Price Index (PPI) rose 0.7% in February, surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of 0.4%. The core PPI, excluding volatile food and energy, increased 0.3%. Year-over-year, both headline and core PPI surged 3.4% and 3.9% respectively, remaining significantly above the Fed's 2% target and reinforcing inflation concerns.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports: Darden Restaurants, Movado Group, Datavault AI
items.summary: Darden Restaurants ($DRI) reported strong Q3 FY26 results with total sales up 5.9% to $3.3 billion and adjusted diluted EPS of $2.95. Movado Group announced fiscal 2026 net sales of $671.3 million and adjusted operating income of $34.8 million. Datavault AI Inc. ($DVLT) posted its first profitable quarter with record revenue growth and reiterated a $200M FY26 revenue target.
